mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at or near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases asset valuations. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate remains in the zero lower bound.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% as of 2020-09-15 indicates sustained accommodation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations where FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window during ZLB periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in unemployment rate (UNRATE falling by > 0.3 percentage points) signals labor market tightening and wage pressure, which increases corporate earnings growth expectations and drives positive equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by more than 0.3 percentage points in the prior month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.9% as of 2020-09-15 reflects post-COVID recovery phase
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or the correlation between monthly UNRATE changes and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth above trend signals robust consumer demand and corporate revenue expansion. When RSAFS year-over-year growth exceeds 3%, equity valuations expand over the next 20 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised upward.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 537,526M as of 2020-08-15 reflects elevated consumer spending during pandemic
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances where RSAFS YoY growth > 3%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) contraction signals supply-side weakness and potential stagflation risk. When INDPRO declines month-over-month, equity investors reduce risk exposure, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as growth concerns dominate.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 101.36 as of 2020-08-15 shows recovery from pandemic trough
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances following monthly INDPRO declines, or the direction accuracy of the signal is below 48% over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal consumer confidence and discretionary spending capacity. When both HOUST and RSAFS are above their 12-month moving averages simultaneously, the interaction indicates broad-based demand strength that drives positive 20-day equity returns through multiplier effects.
candidate_trigger: both HOUST and RSAFS exceed their respective 12-month moving averages
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,416K and RSAFS at 537,526M as of 2020-08-15
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 48% of co-triggered instances, or the joint signal's Rank IC is lower than either individual proxy's IC by more than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth combined with stable or declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals a tight labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings. When PAYEMS grows month-over-month and UNRATE is stable or falling, the interaction predicts positive 60-day SPX returns through sustained demand momentum.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases month-over-month AND UNRATE is stable or declines
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 141,720K and UNRATE at 7.9% as of 2020-09-15
regime_hint: labor market recovery with employment gains
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in fewer than 54% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term's marginal contribution to predictive power is not statistically significant (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 2.5% year-over-year combined with FEDFUNDS remaining below 1% signals monetary policy lag and real rate compression. This regime-conditional interaction predicts negative 20-day SPX returns as investors anticipate future rate hikes and equity repricing.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 260.21 and FEDFUNDS at 0.09% as of 2020-09-15
regime_hint: monetary policy lag with rising inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 52% of co-triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict equity drawdowns in at least 3 of the last 5 instances where inflation accelerates while rates remain low.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly at or above 2.5% annualized signals sustained economic expansion that supports equity valuations. In the post-shock recovery regime, above-trend GDP growth predicts positive 60-day SPX returns as earnings revisions accelerate and risk appetite normalizes.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 17,302.51B as of 2020-04-15 reflects Q1 2020 pandemic impact
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5%, or the signal's predictive power deteriorates below Rank IC of 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.
